Sadashiv Rao was related to the late Maharaja as his:brother 	(b) cousin 	(c) distant nephew 	(d) ministerWhich two neighbouring states tried to wrest Jhansi from Lakshmibai?Delhi and Kanpur 	(b) Datia and Orcha 	(c) Meerut and Lucknow 	(d) Kalpi and GwaliorWhich fort had come into British hands before they planned to attack Jhansi?Fort of Kalpi 	(b) Fort of Chanderi 	(c) Fort of Delhi 	(d) Fort of BithurWhen did British guns begin to bombard Jhansi?1 May 1857 	  	(b) 19 November 1853 	(c) 25 March 1858 	  	(d) 24 May 1858What major event began in 1857?A famine 	  	(b) A war with France 	(c) A revolt against the British 	(d) A royal celebrationWrite ‘T’ for true and ‘F’ for false statements. Manikarnika was the daughter of Moropant. The revolt of 1857 began at Meerut. Women were not allowed to join Lakshmibai’s army. Lakshmibai was fatally wounded in the end. Rao Saheb’s army won easily against the British. The emperor’s sons were treated kindly by the British. Arrange the following events in the order(1-7)   in which they happened in the chapter. Lakshmibai asks her people whether they want to defend Jhansi or make peace with the British.Manu grows up at Bithur, where she studies with the boys and develops a bold spirit.Lakshmibai continues to fight bravely, but she is fatally wounded in battle.After the death of her child, the Maharaja adopts a son, but the British refuse to recognise him as heir.The revolt of 1857 spreads, and Lakshmibai takes control of Jhansi with the support of her people.Manu marries the Maharaja of Jhansi and receives the name Lakshmibai.The British bombard Jhansi, but Lakshmibai inspires her soldiers to fight with courage.Answer the following questions. 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical Thinking“Lakshmibai was not only brave but also a wise ruler.” Justify this statement with examples from the chapter.Describe the challenges Lakshmibai faced from both the British and neighbouring rulers. How did she deal with them?How did Lakshmibai combine compassion with administrative efficiency during her rule?Describe the role of women in Lakshmibai’s army.What happened after Lakshmibai left Jhansi?What mistakes led to the defeat of Rao Saheb’s army?Describe the circumstances leading to Lakshmibai’s death.	Put on Your Thinking Caps	 	/Read the situation carefully and reflect deeply before answering.Imagine you are in a situation where: 	• You know something is wrong (unfair rule, cheating, or bullying)  	• Speaking up might bring consequences (punishment, isolation, criticism) Now answer:What would you choose—silence or speaking up? Why?What could be the short-term consequences of your decision?What could be the long-term impact of your decision?Can courage sometimes be risky? Explain with reasoning.	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Look up the meanings of the words below. Tick the word that best completes the sentence. 	Rani Lakshmibai is remembered as a _________ queen. 	/ benevolent 	/ courageous 	/ diplomatic 	 influentiaStudy the following words from the text: 	seize, sovereign, their, field	“i before e except after c” 	These words contain the letter combinations ‘ie’ and ‘ei’.	 (But remember—there are some exceptions.) 	Examples:believe, receive 	Now, fill in the blanks with ‘ie’ or ‘ei’: 	1. c___ling  	2. bel___ve  3. th___f  4. ach___ve 	5. perc___ve  	6. prot___n  7. soc___ty  8. sh___ld 	9. w___ght  	10. fr___nd 	Grammar Lab	  	/ModalsModals are helping verbs used to express:Obligation (must, have to)•  We must protect our     country. Advice (should, ought to)•  Soldiers should remain     brave. Possibility (may, might, could)•  The British might attack       the fort.Choose the most suitable modal verb from the options given in brackets.We ______ defend our country at all costs. (must / may)	Think whether the sentence The soldiers ______ stay alert at all times. (should / might)	expresses duty, advice Citizens ______ remain calm in times of crisis. (should / might)	or possibility The army ______ face unexpected challenges during war. (may / must)	before choosing the You ______ always stand up against injustice. (should / may)	modal.The commander ______ have a clear plan before taking action. (must / might)The people ______ support their leader in times of need. (should / could)The situation ______ become worse if no action is taken. (may / must)ConnectCommunicate	Listening Skills	 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingFill in the blanks with suitable words from the passage after listening carefully as your teacher reads the passage twice.Rani Abbakka Chowta ruled the kingdom of ____________ in present-day Karnataka.The ____________ were trying to control trade along India's western coast.One of the main reasons for their interest in the region was the profitable __________ trade.  4. Abbakka formed alliances with neighbouring rulers, including the _________ of Calicut.Her forces used small, fast ____________ to surprise enemy ships.Historians remember Rani Abbakka as a symbol of courage, leadership, and resistance against foreign ____________.	Speaking Skills	 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingYour school is facing a serious problem such as bullying, unfair rules or safety issues. You are chosen as a student leader. Will you raise your voice immediately or try to solve it peacefully? Why?Think carefully and speak for 1 minute explaining your decision.	Writing Skills	  	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingImagine you are a soldier in Lakshmibai’s army during the attack on Jhansi. Write a diary entry (120–150 words) describing: 	• the atmosphere during the battle 	• Lakshmibai’s leadership  	• your feelings and fearsScan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.Teacher's Text for Listening SkillsChapter - 1Early one morning, the forest was full of life. Peacocks called from the treetops, rabbits hopped through the bushes, and gazelles grazed peacefully in the shade. Tall trees protected the soil from the hot sun and provided food and shelter to many creatures. Suddenly, the sound of heavy boots and sharp axes echoed through the forest. The animals became restless and began to run. They sensed a danger that humans had not yet noticed.Chapter - 2steplighthardlandturndarkclimbfloorbarefallreachrestChapter - 3Decision-making is an important part of life. Every day, people make choices that affect themselves and others. Some decisions are simple, while others require careful thought. A thoughtful person considers the consequences before acting. Such decisions lead to better outcomes and help build trust and respect in society.Chapter - 4Two friends met for lunch. One friend encouraged the other to order freely. However, the guest decided to order only a small meal. He explained that taking more food than necessary often leads to waste. The host appreciated his thoughtful attitude and agreed with him.Chapter - 7The lottery was conducted—as were the square dances, the teen-age club, the Halloween program— by Mr. Summers, who had time and energy to devote to civic activities. He was a round-faced, jovial man and he ran the coal business, and people were sorry for him, because he had no children and his wife was a scold. When he arrived in the square, carrying the black wooden box, there was a murmur of conversation among the villagers, and he waved and called, “Little late today, folks.” The postmaster, Mr. Graves, followed him, carrying a three-legged stool, and the stool was put in the center of the square and Mr. Summers set the black box down on it. The villagers kept their distance, leaving a space between themselves and the stool, and when Mr. Summers said, “Some of you fellows want to give me a hand?,” there was a hesitation before two men, Mr. Martin and his oldest son, Baxter, came forward to hold the box steady on the stool while Mr. Summers stirred up the papers inside it.Chapter - 9A visitor entered a room and noticed that everyone seemed unusually cheerful. A large umbrella stood near the door, and several pairs of muddy shoes were placed outside. The visitor assumed that the family had just returned from a long journey. Later, he discovered that the shoes belonged to workers repairing the garden and the umbrella had been left by a neighbour.Chapter - 10Human beings are mortal.Sunlight makes the brook sparkle brightly.The brook makes continuous sound while it moves.The river is full of water.The brook flows silently as it passes over a grassy path..Chapter - 11The history of mountaineering includes many remarkable individuals. Bachendri Pal became the first Indian woman to climb Mount Everest. Junko Tabei of Japan became the first woman in the world to reach its summit. Reinhold Messner achieved another extraordinary feat by becoming the first person to climb all fourteen mountains that rise above 8,000 metres. Although their achievements were different, all three demonstrated courage, discipline and perseverance.Chapter - 12Captain Vikram Batra was one of the brave soldiers who served during the Kargil War. Known for his determination and leadership, he successfully completed several challenging missions. His courage inspired fellow soldiers during difficult situations. Even today, his life is remembered as an example of dedication, duty, and service to the nation.
